zh.lifeSummary: 當Otto III von Görtz Tirol und Kärnten Krain於1261年出生在義大利特倫迪諾-阿爾托 阿吉波爾薩諾時，他的父親Meinhard IV von Görtz Tirol23歲，母親Elisabeth von Bayern34歲。他於1297年與Eufemia Piast von Silesia-Liegnitz of Legnica結婚。他們至少是4個女兒的父母。他去世於1310年5月25日在奧地利享年49歲，埋葬在奧地利提洛爾易姆斯特斯塔姆斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Otto III von Görtz Tirol und Kärnten Krain于1261年出生在意大利特倫迪諾-阿爾托 阿吉波爾薩諾时，他的父亲Meinhard IV von Görtz Tirol23岁，母亲Elisabeth von Bayern34岁。他于1297年与Eufemia Piast von Silesia-Liegnitz of Legnica结婚。他们至少是4个女儿的父母。他去世于1310年5月25日在奥地利享年49岁，埋葬在奥地利提洛爾易姆斯特斯塔姆斯。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Görtz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Dieter, Erwin, Gottlieb, Helmut.
East German: habitational name from Golz or Golssen in eastern Germany, placenames that may derive from Sorbian or other Slavic góla, hola, etc. ‘heath’.
Germanized form of Sorbian Gólc: nickname or status name from Lower Sorbian gólc ‘boy, young man’, also ‘apprentice, farmhand’.

variants.name: Golz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Bernhard, Erwin, Friedhelm, Hans, Otto.
German and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): variant of   Goltz   1 and 3.
German (Gölz): see Goelz .

variants.name: Golec
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Stanislaw, Teofil, Tomasz, Zdzislaw.

Polish: nickname from golec, from goły ‘naked’, also ‘penniless’, in various possible senses: it could denote a naked or hairless (bald or clean-shaven) man, and also someone penniless, totally destitute, a naked wretch.

Croatian and Slovenian: nickname from Croatian dialect and Slovenian golec ‘the beardless one’, from gol ‘beardless’ (also ‘naked’), or a topographic name for someone who lived on a treeless ground, derived from gol ‘treeless’. As a nickname it may also have the meaning ‘poor man’ (compare above and Golik ). Compare Goltz .

nameMeanings.name: und
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Runte  .

variants.name: Runte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Erhard.

German: in the northwest of Germany most probably a topographic name from a field name based on run ‘mud’, which gave rise to several placenames in the Rhine-Ruhr area, for example Runten, Runtenberg, Rünthe near Hamm, Westphalia.

nameMeanings.name: Kärnten
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erhardt, Ernst, Hans, Helmut, Horst, Klaus, Orlo, Siegfried.
North German and Dutch: variant of   Carsten  , itself a variant of   Christian  .

variants.name: Kasten
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Kurt, Aloysius, Armin, Arno, Dietrich, Eldor, Ernst, Klaus, Otto, Wilhelm.
North German, East Frisian, North Frisian, and Dutch: from a vernacular variant of the personal name   Karsten  .

German: from Middle High German kasten ‘chest, coffer’ or kornkasten ‘grain bin’, hence a metonymic occupational name for the manager of a granary, and later for the treasurer of an estate or guild. See also Kastner .

variants.name: Carsten
meanings: North German and Dutch: from the personal name Carsten, a variant of Christian . In North America, this surname is also an altered form of the variant Karsten .

variants.name: Karstens
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Michel, Vernice.
North German, Dutch, and Danish: patronymic from   Karsten  . Compare   Carstens  .

nameMeanings.name: Krain
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name from Old Norse grein ‘inlet; fork in a river’, or a habitational name from some place with that or a similar name, such as Grain (Kent).
English: from the Old Norse personal name Grein, literally ‘branch’, but also ‘discord’ or ‘discernment’.

English: from Middle English grain ‘bud, pimple, pustule’ (Old French grain), possibly a nickname for a person with facial marks. From the late 16th century the name was augmented, or possibly superseded, by the name of the Protestant refugees from France and the Low Countries (see 4 below).

nameMeanings.name: Otto
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a short form of any of the various Germanic compound personal names containing the element od, ot ‘prosperity, wealth’ ( compare the corresponding Old English ēad in names such as Edward and Edwin ). St Otto of Bamberg ( d. 1139 ) was a missionary to the Pomeranians. Otto the Great ( 912–73 ) is generally regarded as the founder of the Holy Roman Empire, and the name has been borne by several members of German and Austrian royal houses. It was recorded occasionally among immigrants to England from the Low Countries in the 14th century, but failed to establish itself. Since the 19th century it has again been used occasionally in the English-speaking world, mostly among immigrants from Germanic countries.

nameMeanings.name: III
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

en.lifeSummary: When Otto III von Görtz Tirol und Kärnten Krain was born in 1261, in Bolzano, Trentino-Alto Adige, Italy, his father, Meinhard IV von Görtz Tirol, was 23 and his mother, Elisabeth von Bayern, was 34. He married Eufemia Piast von Silesia-Liegnitz of Legnica in 1297. They were the parents of at least 4 daughters. He died on 25 May 1310, in Austria, at the age of 49, and was buried in Stams, Imst, Tirol, Austria.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Otto III von Görtz Tirol und Kärnten Krain est né en 1261, à Bolzano, Trentino-Alto Adige, Italie, son père, Meinhard IV von Görtz Tirol, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Elisabeth von Bayern, avait 34 ans. Il a épousé Eufemia Piast von Silesia-Liegnitz of Legnica en 1297. Ils ont eu au moins 4 filles. Il est décédé le 25 mai 1310, à Autriche, à l’âge de 49 ans, et a été enterré à Stams, Imst, Tirol, Autriche.

de.Greiferburg, Spittal An Der Drau, Karnte: Spittal an der Drau, Spittal an der Drau, Kärnten, Österreich
de.lifeSummary: Als Otto III von Görtz Tirol und Kärnten Krain 1261 in Bolzano, Trentino-Alto Adige, Italien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Meinhard IV von Görtz Tirol 23 und seine Mutter Elisabeth von Bayern 34. Er hat 1297 Eufemia Piast von Silesia-Liegnitz of Legnica geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Töchter. Er starb am 25. Mai 1310 im Alter von 49 Jahren in Österreich und wurde in Stams, Imst, Tirol, Österreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Otto III von Görtz Tirol und Kärnten Krain è nato il nel 1261 nel Bolzano, Trentino-Alto Adige, Italia, il suo padre, Meinhard IV von Görtz Tirol, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Elisabeth von Bayern, aveva 34 anni. Lui sposato Eufemia Piast von Silesia-Liegnitz of Legnica nel 1297. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figlie. Morì il 25 maggio 1310, nel Austria, all'età di 49 anni, e fu sepolto a Stams, Imst, Tirolo, Austria.

ja.lifeSummary: Otto III von Görtz Tirol und Kärnten Krainが1261年にイタリアトレンティノ-アルトー アディッジボルザノで生まれた時，父Meinhard IV von Görtz Tirolは23歳，母Elisabeth von Bayernは34歳であった。1297年にEufemia Piast von Silesia-Liegnitz of Legnicaと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の娘の親であった。彼は1310年5月25日にオーストリアで49歳で死去し，オーストリアティロルイムストスタムズにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Otto III von Görtz Tirol und Kärnten Krain(이)가 1261년 이탈리아 트렌틴-알토 아디지 볼자노에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Meinhard IV von Görtz Tirol(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Elisabeth von Bayern(은)는 34세였다. 그는 1297년 Eufemia Piast von Silesia-Liegnitz of Legnica(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1310년 5월 25일 오스트리아에서 49세의 나이로 사망했으며 오스트리아 티럴 임스트 스탬에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Otto III von Görtz Tirol und Kärnten Krain nasceu em 1261, em Bolzano, Trentino-Alto Adige, Itália, seu pai, Meinhard IV von Görtz Tirol, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Elisabeth von Bayern, tinha 34 anos. Ele casou-se com Eufemia Piast von Silesia-Liegnitz of Legnica em 1297. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Otto III von Görtz Tirol und Kärnten Krain родился в 1261 г. в местности болзано, трентино-алто адидж, Италия, его отцу по имени Meinhard IV von Görtz Tirol было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Elisabeth von Bayern, 34. Он женился на женщине по имени Eufemia Piast von Silesia-Liegnitz of Legnica в 1297 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 дочери. Он умер 25 мая 1310 г. в местности Австрия в возрасте 49 лет и был похоронен в местности стамс, имст, тирол, Австрия.
